stage.name: Final checkpoint: CVA6 From Zero
stage.description: A series-level checkpoint for the first complete CVA6 From Zero learning path.
stage.courseTemplateName: Professional Technical Deep Dive - Evidence-Grounded SlideKit

scenes.title: CVA6 From Zero now has a complete first path

content.title: CVA6 From Zero now has a complete first path
content.subtitle: The final checkpoint checks whether the learner can classify claims, choose sources, and keep boundaries precise.
content.thesis: The goal is not memorization. The goal is competent, source-grounded CVA6 reading.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 documentation overview
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The official documentation separates user guide, requirements, design documents, configuration-specific manuals, and APU material.

content.speakerNotes: This lesson is Final checkpoint: CVA6 From Zero. The final checkpoint checks whether the learner can classify claims, choose sources, and keep boundaries precise. The point is not to memorize a wall of CVA6 facts. The point is to build a disciplined source-reading habit: name the boundary, name the source, and avoid claims that the public material does not support.

actions.text: This lesson is Final checkpoint: CVA6 From Zero. The final checkpoint checks whether the learner can classify claims, choose sources, and keep boundaries precise. The point is not to memorize a wall of CVA6 facts. The point is to build a disciplined source-reading habit: name the boundary, name the source, and avoid claims that the public material does not support.

scenes.title: The full learning path in one map

content.title: From what CVA6 is to how to read it yourself
content.subtitle: The course is a sequence of boundary decisions.

cards.title: Units 01-02
cards.body: Positioning, source roles, family variants, and configuration literacy.

cards.title: Units 03-04
cards.body: Pipeline spine, frontend, PC selection, prediction, and redirects.

cards.title: Units 05-07
cards.body: Issue, scoreboard, execute, commit, LSU, MMU, and memory ordering.

cards.title: Units 08-09
cards.body: Interfaces, integration, source reading, verification handoff, and capstone workflow.

content.takeaway: CVA6 reading is a staged skill, not one giant download.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 documentation overview
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The official documentation separates user guide, requirements, design documents, configuration-specific manuals, and APU material.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 public repository source tree
sourceAnchors.locator: repository directories core, docs, corev_apu, verif, config, util
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The public repository is organized around core RTL, documentation, APU/testharness material, verification, configuration, and tooling directories.

content.speakerNotes: This final checkpoint closes the first full path. We started with what CVA6 is and what it is not. Then we learned configuration, pipeline structure, frontend behavior, scoreboard, execute, commit, memory, interfaces, and finally source-reading workflow. The path is not everything about CVA6, but it is enough to stop being lost in the repository.

actions.text: This final checkpoint closes the first full path. We started with what CVA6 is and what it is not. Then we learned configuration, pipeline structure, frontend behavior, scoreboard, execute, commit, memory, interfaces, and finally source-reading workflow. The path is not everything about CVA6, but it is enough to stop being lost in the repository.

scenes.title: Five rules for serious CVA6 explanations

content.title: The rules are simple; following them is the craft

items.title: Name the boundary
items.body: Core, configuration, memory, interface, SoC, or verification.

items.title: Name the source
items.body: Requirement, user manual, design doc, RTL, or testharness.

items.title: Name the check
items.body: How would this claim be observed or tested?

content.takeaway: Public technical teaching is disciplined source work.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 requirements: scope and verification intent
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The requirements document defines the CVA6 feature scope and ties requirements to verification intent and interface requirements.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 public repository source tree
sourceAnchors.locator: repository directories core, docs, corev_apu, verif, config, util
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The public repository is organized around core RTL, documentation, APU/testharness material, verification, configuration, and tooling directories.

content.speakerNotes: Here is the final rule set. Name the boundary. Name the source. Name the scope. Name the check. If a learner can do those four things, they can produce technical explanations that are useful and honest. That is the foundation for future OpenHW Explorer courses as well.

actions.text: Here is the final rule set. Name the boundary. Name the source. Name the scope. Name the check. If a learner can do those four things, they can produce technical explanations that are useful and honest. That is the foundation for future OpenHW Explorer courses as well.

questions.question: What is CVA6 best described as in this course?

options.label: A configurable application-class RISC-V core family/source base
A finished SoC with every peripheral included inside the core

questions.analysis: The course repeatedly separates CVA6 core/family positioning from SoC-level claims.

questions.question: Why does configuration literacy matter?

options.label: Because CVA6 claims can change by variant and parameter
Because it removes all need for source reading
Because it proves every branch is identical

questions.analysis: Variant and parameter scope are central to safe public claims.

questions.question: Which stages or concepts belong to the core pipeline reading spine?
questions.analysis: AXI fabric is an interface/system topic, not a pipeline stage.

questions.question: What does the scoreboard mainly help preserve?

options.label: Instruction identity and result tracking until commit can consume completed work

questions.analysis: The scoreboard keeps backend instruction identity and returned result state.

questions.question: What is the safest explanation of stores?

options.label: They carry side effects and require commit discipline
They are always identical to loads

questions.analysis: Stores are architecturally dangerous because they can change memory state.

questions.question: Which Unit 08 boundaries should stay separate?
questions.analysis: All four are separate integration zones.

questions.question: What should a source-backed final answer include?

options.label: Claim, evidence, scope, and checkable question

questions.analysis: This is the source-reading workflow from Unit 09.

questions.question: What is the best final learning outcome?

options.label: Read CVA6 sources with boundaries, evidence, and verification-minded questions
Memorize every signal name without context
Assume all integration behavior is identical

questions.analysis: The course is about competent, source-grounded reading.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 documentation overview
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The official documentation separates user guide, requirements, design documents, configuration-specific manuals, and APU material.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 public repository source tree
sourceAnchors.locator: repository directories core, docs, corev_apu, verif, config, util
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The public repository is organized around core RTL, documentation, APU/testharness material, verification, configuration, and tooling directories.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Scoreboard submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The scoreboard tracks issued instructions, stores returned results by transaction ID, and exposes the oldest completed entry to commit.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: COMMIT_STAGE updates architectural state, writes CSR/register state, commits stores, and handles precise traps in order.

content.speakerNotes: This quiz checks the core judgment of Final checkpoint: CVA6 From Zero. The right answers should feel less like trivia and more like disciplined classification: what is the boundary, what is the source, what is the limitation, and what would we check next?

actions.text: This quiz checks the core judgment of Final checkpoint: CVA6 From Zero. The right answers should feel less like trivia and more like disciplined classification: what is the boundary, what is the source, what is the limitation, and what would we check next?

scenes.title: Summary: Final checkpoint: CVA6 From Zero

content.title: Final checkpoint: CVA6 From Zero
takeaways: Demonstrate readiness to read CVA6 sources and produce scoped technical explanations.
Turn every public explanation into an evidence-backed learning artifact.
content.nextStep: CVA6 From Zero first edition is complete. Future work can deepen selected lessons and add Chinese versions after the English base stabilizes.
content.closing: This is how OpenHW Explorer can teach serious open hardware without drifting into unsupported certainty.

content.speakerNotes: To summarize: Demonstrate readiness to read CVA6 sources and produce scoped technical explanations. Keep the claim small, keep the source visible, and keep the boundary honest. CVA6 From Zero first edition is complete. Future work can deepen selected lessons and add Chinese versions after the English base stabilizes.
